def: adj. S. सम्मुख Present; before one's face (آگے 1, 2, 3.). 1. Nā pūchhā unse 'kaun ho tum,' na apne jī kī bāt kahī, Na karnā kuchh inkār paṛā, na kahnā ṭhairā yoṅ hī sahī, Jab chhoṛī khuāhish bose kī, phir kāheko dushnām sahī, Jab sanmukh ho gaë chanchal ke sab chhor havas yeh bāt rahī, Ṭuk dekh liyā, dil shād kīyā, khush-waqt hue aur chal nikle . Pure love. Nazīr. I asked not 'Maid, who are you, say,' nor did my heart betray, I'd naught to say, could she say nay? so be it as it may, The wish to kiss her cast away, why hear abuse foul, pray? When come before that sprightly fay all lust subdued, each day One look, my heart made glad, I blessed my stars, and took my way. 2. Shām ghaṭā ek nār kahāve, Sukh ke samaë voh kām na dve; Dukh ke samaë voh san-mukh hove, Aisī nār na dekhī hove . Riddle. Ans. Ḍhāl shield. A female called, her body black, in peace and joy her none may lack, The front of evil she 'll attack, you never saw this female's back. sanmukh fauj chalānā , v. a. To march against; to attack. Kahai Dashānan sunahu subhattā, Mardahu bhālu kapin kar ṭhaṭṭā, Hauṅ marihauṅ bhūp dou bhāī, As kahī sanmukh fauj chalāī . Rāmāyan. sanmukh k. , v. a. To bring face to face; confront. sanmukh h. , v. n. To face; come in front.

def: s.m. Depositing, delivering over, entrusting to; resigning, relinquishing, abdication; abandonment of the world, renunciation of temporal concerns.
s.m. One who abandons or resigns; one who has abandoned all worldly possessions and affections, an ascetic, a devotee; a Brāhmaṇ of the fourth order or āśram, a religious mendicant.
more: n. m. A Hindu of the 4th order. The last state of a Brahmin who has renounced all worldly affections and possessions and lives by mendicancy. He is usually a worshipper of Shiv. 1. Sannyāsī paisā nahīṅ chhūte . The devotee will never touch money. 2. Nār marī, ghar sampat nāsī, Mūṅṛ muṛāë bhaë sanyāsī . The wife deceased, his money fled, Sanniyāsī see with shaven head. 3. Sarb dharm se pare Bed meṅ likhā hai, sun, sannyās kā dharm, Kyā koī jāne panḍit ke Sannyāsī kā hai kun sā karm? Lāunī. 'Tis written in the Veds the ascetic's is the holiest life, What Pandit e'er could comprehend Sannyasi's life and strife?
s. m. A Brāhmaṇ of the fourth order, a religious mendicant.

def: adj. S. समष्टि 1. Collected. 2. Ready. 1. Woh bhī laṛne ko sāoṅṭā ho gayā . 2. Pāk sāṅmṭhā jib guṛ hogā, baiṭh sukhālī khāūṅgī, Maiṅ īkh khet māṅ jāūṅgī . W. Rus. song. When boiled the sugar is and done, I'll sit and eat it all anon. Chorus . Straight to the cane field I'll be gone. 3. Convalescent; well; in health. sāoṅṭhā k , v. a. To collect together. sāṅoṭhā h. , v. n. 1. To be collected or gathered. 2. To be ready, prepared. 3. To square or stand up to one; to defy.

more: n. m. S. सर्प Pr. sappo . A snake; serpent. 1. Sāṅp kā kāthā rassī se ḍare! Prov. The snake-bitten dreads every rope. 2. Sāṅp mare, na lāṭhī ṭūṭe . Prov. Nor kill a snake, nor break your stick. 3. Sāṅp nikal gayā, lakīr piṭā karo! Prov. The snake escaped, the stick lay on his trail! 4. Ham sāṅp nahīṅ haiṅ ki jīyeṅ chāṭ-ke miṭṭī . Prov. I am not a snake that I can lick the dust and live. (Human beings must have proper food.) 5. Sāṅp ke muṅh meṅ chhachūṅdar; nigle to andhā, ugte to koṛhī . Prov. A muskrat in the snake's mouth; if he swallows it he will be blind, if he vomit it he'll become a leper. 6. Sāṅpoṅ kī sabhā meṅ jīboṅ kī lapālap . Prov. You know the snakes' bickering, By the long tongues flickering. 7. Dharm saneh ubhai mati gherī, Bhaī gatī sāṅp chhachhūṅdar kerī . Rāmāyan. 8. Sāṅp kā bachcha sapoliyā . Prov. From snakes but snakes are born. sāṅp sūṅghnā ; Sant. biṅgger , v. n. l…
s. m. A snake, a serpent.
subs: (or ḍartā hai), prov. 'One who has been bitten by a snake dreads a piece of rope'; a burnt child dreads the fire.

def: s.m. Doubt, misgiving, anxiety; fear, apprehension; imagination; reflection.
more: n. m. S. संशय 1. Anxiety; care. 1. Rotī kā bhī sāṅsā hai . 2. Jab hāth meṅ līyā kāṅsā, tau roṭiyoṅ kā kyā. sāṅsā? Prov. When in your hand you 've taken the cup then care for daily bread give up. 3. Beṭī javān huī aur uske biyāh kā sāṅsā chaṛhā . 4. Samman, sāṅsā mat karo! sir par hai sāīṅ, Jo kuchh likhā lilāṭ meṅ, bhejeṅge yāṅhīṅ . O Samman fret not! o'er your head's the Lord of might, Whate'er predestined is He 'll send you here aright. 5. Sāsaṛ! sāṅsā mat kare, dekh thuṛeṛā kām! Thoṛe ko bohatā kare, den lage jab Rām . Wom. Rus. song. Samajh samajh ban charnā, hirnā! ek ban charnā, dūje ban phirnā! phande kisī ke na paṛnā! Bāp kahe 'merā pūt sapūtā,' bahin kahe 'merā bīran,' Yeh sansār babūl kā kāṅṭā, nirakh nīrakh pag dharnā . Hymn. Bhajan . Feed cautiously! O fallow deer, upon the forests free! Graze here, rove there, beware of falling in a snare! The father says 'This is my goodly son,' the sister says 'this is my brother own.' This world is but a prickly Acacia brake, look well before a step you take! 4. Tulsī! yā sansār meṅ pāṅch ratan haiṅ sār, Sādhū milan aur Har bhajan, dayā, dharam, upkār . O Tulsī! in this world five jewels all the rest exceed, Saints' company, Har's worship, pity, faith, and virtuous deed.

def: v.t. To prepare, to construct; to dress, decorate, adorn, deck; to arrange, methodize, regulate, rectify, correct, mend, improve.

more: n. f. 1. Amendment ( durustī , 4—6 and islāh ). Mār pīchhe saṅvār hī hai? First mar, then make! (First slay, then raise to life?). 2. Wom. Curse; malediction. Allāh-māre, tujh ko Khudā kī saṅvār ho! Mah. Wom. O heaven-accursed! God mend you (destroy you).

ety: saṅwār˚ = Prk. संवारे(इ) = S. संवारय (ति), caus. of rt. सम्+वृ
def: v.t. To prepare, to construct; to dress, decorate, adorn, deck; to arrange, methodize, regulate, rectify, correct, mend, improve.
more: v. a. 1. See ārāstah k . and ṭhīk k. , (2—4). 1. Koī sakhī laṅgaṭe, koī lugava saṅvarate, Ahe jāit gelan āpan āpan gharāvā re kī . Mag. Shiv's marriage. Some women naked were, and some their sheets about them drew, In sooth they one and all to their own houses quick withdrew. ☞ While the women were abusing the loathsome form in which the god bridegroom appeared, he caused serpents to appear under their clothes which they hastily threw off and ran naked to their homes. 2. Merī bigrī ko saṅvāro , Rām! Relieve my misery, O Ram! 2. To correct; amend; improve. Bolo, pyāre, Sītā Rām! Rām saṅvāre sab-kā kām! Mendicants. Call, friend, on Sītā Ram! who all things does for you, his name proclaim!
(caus. of سنورنا) v. a. To prepare, dress, deck, decorate, adjust, adorn, arrange, accomplish.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Of a dark or sallow complexion; dark, swarthy; sallow, brown, nut-brown; of handsome countenance; an epithet of Krishṇ

Sāṅvrī dekh-ke sūrat kisī matvālī kī, Go musalmān hūṅ, magar bolūṅgā 'jai Kālī kī! ' Whene 'er a nut-brown maid with eyes intoxicate I chance to see, Although a Moslem I'm constrained to cry 'to Kālī glory be!' sāṅvlā, yā sāṅvrā salonā, sāṅvlī salonī , adj. 1. Brown and saltish. 2. Rich brown. Sāṅvle salone haiṅ sharbat ko! (Fālsā grewia asiatica , Wat.) Delhi street cry. sāṅvrā, sāṅvalyā, sāṅolā , n. m. An epithet of Krishna.
